tool_memories.question: How many patients do we have?
tool_memories.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_patients FROM patients

tool_memories.question: List all doctors and their specializations
tool_memories.sql: SELECT name, specialization FROM doctors ORDER BY name

tool_memories.question: Show me appointments for last month
tool_memories.sql: SELECT a.id, p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name AS patient_name, d.name AS doctor_name, a.appointment_date, a.status FROM appointments a JOIN patients p ON p.id = a.patient_id JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id WHERE date(a.appointment_date) >= date('now', 'start of month', '-1 month') AND date(a.appointment_date) < date('now', 'start of month') ORDER BY a.appointment_date DESC

tool_memories.question: Which doctor has the most appointments?
tool_memories.sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) AS appointment_count FROM appointments a JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY appointment_count DESC, d.name ASC LIMIT 1

tool_memories.question: What is the total revenue?
tool_memories.sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(total_amount), 2) AS total_revenue FROM invoices

tool_memories.question: Show revenue by doctor
tool_memories.sql: SELECT d.name, ROUND(SUM(t.cost), 2) AS total_revenue FROM treatments t JOIN appointments a ON a.id = t.appointment_id JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC, d.name ASC

tool_memories.question: How many cancelled appointments last quarter?
tool_memories.sql: WITH current_period AS ( SELECT CAST(strftime('%Y', 'now') AS INTEGER) AS current_year, CAST(((CAST(strftime('%m', 'now') AS INTEGER) - 1) / 3) + 1 AS INTEGER) AS current_quarter ), last_period AS ( SELECT CASE WHEN current_quarter = 1 THEN current_year - 1 ELSE current_year END AS target_year, CASE WHEN current_quarter = 1 THEN 4 ELSE current_quarter - 1 END AS target_quarter FROM current_period ) SELECT COUNT(*) AS cancelled_appointments FROM appointments WHERE status = 'Cancelled' AND CAST(strftime('%Y', appointment_date) AS INTEGER) = (SELECT target_year FROM last_period) AND CAST(((CAST(strftime('%m', appointment_date) AS INTEGER) - 1) / 3) + 1 AS INTEGER) = (SELECT target_quarter FROM last_period)

tool_memories.question: Top 5 patients by spending
tool_memories.sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, ROUND(SUM(i.total_amount), 2) AS total_spending FROM invoices i JOIN patients p ON p.id = i.patient_id GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name ORDER BY total_spending DESC, p.last_name ASC, p.first_name ASC LIMIT 5

tool_memories.question: Average treatment cost by specialization
tool_memories.sql: SELECT d.specialization, ROUND(AVG(t.cost), 2) AS average_treatment_cost FROM treatments t JOIN appointments a ON a.id = t.appointment_id JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.specialization ORDER BY average_treatment_cost DESC, d.specialization ASC

tool_memories.question: Show monthly appointment count for the past 6 months
tool_memories.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', appointment_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS appointment_count FROM appointments WHERE date(appointment_date) >= date('now', 'start of month', '-5 months') GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', appointment_date) ORDER BY month ASC

tool_memories.question: Which city has the most patients?
tool_memories.sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS patient_count FROM patients GROUP BY city ORDER BY patient_count DESC, city ASC LIMIT 1

tool_memories.question: List patients who visited more than 3 times
tool_memories.sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, COUNT(*) AS visit_count FROM appointments a JOIN patients p ON p.id = a.patient_id GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 3 ORDER BY visit_count DESC, p.last_name ASC, p.first_name ASC

tool_memories.question: Show unpaid invoices
tool_memories.sql: SELECT i.id, p.first_name, p.last_name, i.invoice_date, i.total_amount, i.paid_amount, i.status FROM invoices i JOIN patients p ON p.id = i.patient_id WHERE i.status IN ('Pending', 'Overdue') ORDER BY i.invoice_date DESC, i.id DESC

tool_memories.question: What percentage of appointments are no-shows?
tool_memories.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'No-Show' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS no_show_percentage FROM appointments

tool_memories.question: Show the busiest day of the week for appointments
tool_memories.sql: SELECT CASE strftime('%w', appointment_date) WHEN '0' THEN 'Sunday' WHEN '1' THEN 'Monday' WHEN '2' THEN 'Tuesday' WHEN '3' THEN 'Wednesday' WHEN '4' THEN 'Thursday' WHEN '5' THEN 'Friday' WHEN '6' THEN 'Saturday' END AS day_of_week, COUNT(*) AS appointment_count FROM appointments GROUP BY strftime('%w', appointment_date) ORDER BY appointment_count DESC, day_of_week ASC LIMIT 1

tool_memories.question: Revenue trend by month
tool_memories.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', invoice_date) AS month, ROUND(SUM(total_amount), 2) AS revenue FROM invoices GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', invoice_date) ORDER BY month ASC

tool_memories.question: Average appointment duration by doctor
tool_memories.sql: SELECT d.name, ROUND(AVG(t.duration_minutes), 2) AS average_duration_minutes FROM treatments t JOIN appointments a ON a.id = t.appointment_id JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY average_duration_minutes DESC, d.name ASC

tool_memories.question: List patients with overdue invoices
tool_memories.sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.first_name, p.last_name, p.city, i.invoice_date, i.total_amount, i.paid_amount FROM invoices i JOIN patients p ON p.id = i.patient_id WHERE i.status = 'Overdue' ORDER BY i.invoice_date DESC, p.last_name ASC, p.first_name ASC

tool_memories.question: Compare revenue between departments
tool_memories.sql: SELECT d.department, ROUND(SUM(t.cost), 2) AS department_revenue FROM treatments t JOIN appointments a ON a.id = t.appointment_id JOIN doctors d ON d.id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.department ORDER BY department_revenue DESC, d.department ASC

tool_memories.question: Show patient registration trend by month
tool_memories.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', registered_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS registrations FROM patients GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', registered_date) ORDER BY month ASC

text_memories: patients table tracks demographics with columns id, first_name, last_name, email, phone, date_of_birth, gender, city, and registered_date.
doctors table contains id, name, specialization, department, and phone. Appointments join to doctors through appointments.doctor_id = doctors.id.
appointments table links patients and doctors using patient_id and doctor_id. Statuses are Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled, and No-Show.
treatments table links to appointments using appointment_id. Use treatments.cost for procedure-level revenue and treatments.duration_minutes for duration analysis.
invoices table links to patients, not directly to doctors. Use invoices.total_amount, paid_amount, status, and invoice_date for billing and revenue trend questions.
For SQLite month trends, use strftime('%Y-%m', date_column). For day-of-week analysis, use strftime('%w', appointment_date) and map 0-6 to Sunday-Saturday.
